notes.note: Pompey's estate in the Alban Hills, south of Rome; one of his favoured residences when not at the city or his Cumae villa.

Publicani: equestrian companies that bought from the censors the right to collect Rome's taxes. Crassus was their patron in the Senate; the consular pair settling the books in person is a sign of how the joint consulship was run.

Most likely the games associated with the dedication of Pompey's theatre and the temple of Venus Victrix in 55 BC --- the first stone theatre in Rome --- though the date of dedication itself is debated.
Marcus Pomponius Dionysius, freedman of Atticus and tutor to the young Cicero and Quintus; here Cicero's reading-companion at Cumae.
Illa populi Appuleia: a play on the gens Appuleia and the populus Romanus --- jocular shorthand for the Clodian/Appian rabble Atticus deals with in Rome.
Greek polymath and writer of biographical and bibliographical works; here a slave or freedman bearing the name, sent as a courier so that Atticus will always have a man at hand to bring back letters.
